semanticCore: Melting, shaking, liquid flow, path swerve, or dynamic sway
quranicRelevance: Tectonic shaking/swaying of the earth (Maara - 'an tameeda bikum'), and violent swaying/swirling of skies (Mawr - 'yawma tamooru as-sama'u mawran').

keyDerivations.meaning: To shake, sway, swing, rock, or bend
To sway, swirl, stir, flow, or move dynamically

figurativeOrRareUsages: يَوْمَ تَمُورُ السَّمَاءُ مَوْرًا (52:9): The day the sky will sway/swirl violently (the cosmic collapse of celestial order).
أَنْ تَمِيدَ بِكُمْ: Lest it shake/sway with you (mountains acting as stabilizers against structural rolling).
discardedNoise: Obscure details of camel walking sways.
summaryEn: The root denotes dynamic swaying and swirling, describing the stabilization of the earth against rolling (Myd) and the violent swirling (Mawr) of skies at judgment.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على الاضطراب والحركة المائجة، واصفاً حماية الأرض من الترنح (تميد)، والاضطراب الفلكي المزلزل للسماء (تمور).
rawText: مَأَى 1 (like مَاءَ) It (a cat) mewed. (TA, voce مَاءَ, art. موأ.)
